AbbreviationMap.description:
Document-level map from full rendered strings to their abbreviations.

Keys are the full rendered string (exact, case-sensitive). Values are the
replacement abbreviation. Applied after value extraction, before output.

Accepts both `abbreviation-map` (YAML frontmatter) and `abbreviation_map` forms.

oneOf.description: Use the localized term for "and" (e.g., "and" in English).
Use the localized symbol for "and" (e.g., "&" in English).
No conjunction (e.g., "Smith, Jones").

AndOtherOptions.description: How to render "and others" / et al.

oneOf.description: Parse annotation as djot inline markup (default).
Treat annotation as plain text with no markup interpretation.
Parse annotation as org-mode markup.

BibliographyGroup.description:
A bibliography group with selector, optional heading, and per-group sorting.

Groups allow styles to divide bibliographies into labeled sections with
distinct sorting rules. Items match the first group whose selector evaluates
to true (first-match semantics).

# Examples

```yaml
groups:
  - id: vietnamese
    heading:
      localized:
        vi: "Tài liệu tiếng Việt"
        en-US: "Vietnamese Sources"
    selector:
      field:
        language: vi
    sort:
      template:
        - key: author
          sort-order: given-family
```

disambiguate.description:
Optional disambiguation scope.
- `globally` (default): Year suffixes are assigned across the whole bibliography.
- `locally`: Year suffixes are assigned independently within this group.

heading.description:
Optional heading to display above this group.
Omit for no heading (e.g., fallback group).

sort.description:
Optional per-group sorting specification.
Falls back to global bibliography sort if omitted.

template.description:
Optional per-group template override.
Falls back to global bibliography template if omitted.

anyOf.description: Locale general term key resolved at render time.

localized.description: Map keyed by BCP 47 locale identifiers or language tags.

BibliographyPartitionHeading.description: Localizable heading source for automatic bibliography partition sections.

BibliographyPartitionKind.description: Partition key source for bibliography partitioning.

oneOf.description: Partition by Unicode script detected from author/editor/title sort text.
Partition by the reference's effective item language.
Sort a flat bibliography by partition before normal sort keys.
Render visible sections for grouped bibliography output only.
Sort flat output by partition and render visible sections for grouped output.

BibliographySortPartitioning.description: Bibliography partitioning policy for multilingual sort order and sections.

by.description: Source used to derive each reference's partition key.

headings.description: Optional headings for visible partition sections.

mode.description: Whether partitioning affects flat sorting, visible sections, or both.

order.description: Preferred partition order. Unlisted partitions sort after listed ones.

oneOf.description: Match only references cited in the document.
Match all references regardless of citation status.
Abbreviated month + day + year in US order: "Jan 15, 2024".

DelimiterPrecedesLast.description: When to use delimiter before last contributor.

DisambiguationScope.description: Scope for disambiguation (e.g., year suffix assignment).

oneOf.description: Disambiguate across all items in the bibliography.
Disambiguate only within the current group.

DocumentIntegralNameOverride.description: Document-level integral-name override parsed from frontmatter.

contexts.description: Which document contexts participate in the policy.

enabled.description: Whether the integral-name policy is enabled for this document.

subsequent-form.description: The contributor form used after the first mention.

DocumentOptions.description:
Document-level configuration for rendering.

Controls rendering behavior that belongs to the document rather than the style.

abbreviation_map.description: Map from full rendered strings to their abbreviations.

annotation_format.description: Format for annotation text (djot, plain, org).

annotations.description: Reference ID to annotation text mapping.

bibliography_groups.description: Override or replace style-defined bibliography grouping.

inject_ast_indices.description: Whether to annotate semantic wrappers with source template indices.

show_semantics.description: Whether to output semantic markup (HTML spans, Djot attributes).

sort_partitioning.description: Automatic bibliography partitioning by script or language.

GeneralTerm.description:
A list of general terms for citation formatting.

These are the standard terms that appear in bibliographies and citations,
including prepositions (in, at, from, by), punctuation terms (and, et al),
date-related terms (accessed, no-date, circa), locator terms (page, chapter, volume),
and special phrases (ibid, forthcoming, available-at).

oneOf.description: The preposition "in" (e.g., "in Smith, 2020").
The term used for access dates (e.g., "accessed May 1").
The term used for retrieval statements (e.g., "retrieved from URL").
The preposition "at" (e.g., "at the conference").
The preposition "from" (e.g., "from the publisher").
The preposition "of" (e.g., "special issue of").
The preposition "to" (e.g., "from x to y").
The preposition "by" (e.g., "by John Smith").
The term used when no date is available (e.g., "n.d.").
The term used for anonymous authorship (e.g., "anonymous").
The term used for approximate dates (e.g., "circa").
The phrase used for availability statements (e.g., "available at URL").
The term used for immediately repeated citations (e.g., "ibid.").
The conjunction "and" (e.g., "Smith and Jones").
The abbreviation for omitted additional names (e.g., "et al.").
The phrase "and others" (generic use).
The term used for forthcoming works (e.g., "forthcoming").
The term used for online resources (e.g., "online").
The adverb "here".
The term used for deposited materials.
The phrase used to introduce reviewed works (e.g., "review of").
The phrase used for original publication references (e.g., "originally published").
The term used for patents (e.g., "patent").
The general term for volume locators (e.g., "volume", "vol.").
The general term for issue locators (e.g., "issue", "no.").
The general term for page locators (e.g., "page", "p.", "pp.").
The general term for chapter locators (e.g., "chapter", "ch.").
The general term for editions (e.g., "edition", "ed.").
The general term for section locators (e.g., "section", "§").
The label for personal communications (e.g., "personal communication").

GrammaticalGender.description: Grammatical gender used for locale agreement and term selection.

oneOf.description: Masculine grammatical gender.
Feminine grammatical gender.
Neuter grammatical gender.
Common or shared grammatical gender.

anyOf.description: Locale general term key resolved at render time.

localized.description: Map keyed by BCP 47 locale identifiers or language tags.

GroupHeading.description: Localizable heading source for bibliography groups.

GroupSelector.description:
Selector predicate for matching references to groups.

All specified conditions must match (AND logic).
Use the `not` field for negation-based fallback groups.

field.description: Match references by field values (e.g., language, keywords).

not.description:
Negation for fallback groups.
Matches references that do NOT match the nested selector.

GroupSort.description:
Per-group sorting specification.

Sorting follows a template of sort keys, applied in order.
The first key is the primary sort, second is the tiebreaker, etc.

template.description: Ordered list of sort keys to apply.

GroupSortEntry.description: Citation sort configuration: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

GroupSortKey.description: A single sort key in a group sorting template.

key.description: The field or variable to sort by.

order.description:
For type-based ordering: explicit type sequence.

Example: `["legal-case", "statute", "treaty"]` for Bluebook hierarchy.
Items appear in this order regardless of alphabetical content.

sort-order.description:
For name-based sorting: culturally appropriate name order.

Example: `given-family` for Vietnamese, `family-given` for Western.

IntegralNameContexts.description: Which document contexts participate in integral citation name memory.

oneOf.description: Only body-text integral citations participate.
Body text and note citations both participate.

IntegralNameScope.description: The scope where integral citation name-memory resets.

oneOf.description: Keep one name-memory scope for the whole document.
Reset name memory at chapter boundaries.
Reset name memory at section boundaries.

LabelPlacement.description: Label placement relative to contributor names.

oneOf.description: Link the title component.
Link the URL component itself.
Link the DOI component itself.
Link the specific component this config is attached to.
Link the entire bibliography entry.

anchor.description: What text should be hyperlinked (title, url, etc.).

doi.description: Link value to the item's DOI.

target.description: The target for the link (url, doi, etc.).

url.description: Link value to the item's URL.

NameForm.description:
How to render the given-name component of a contributor name.

Controls whether full given names, family name only, or initialized
given names are rendered. Used to express first/subsequent mention
differences (Chicago) and integral/non-integral differences.

Initialization formatting details (`initialize_with`, `initialize_with_hyphen`)
are separate fields and only take effect when `NameForm::Initials` is active.

oneOf.description: Render full given names: "John D. Smith".

Render family name only, suppressing given names: "Smith".
Used for subsequent mentions in Chicago/Turabian note styles.

Render initialized given names using `initialize_with` separator.
If `initialize_with` is None, defaults to ". " (e.g., "J. Smith").
Empty string gives compact initials: "JD Smith".
Display as "Given Family" (e.g., "John Smith").
Display as "Family, Given" (e.g., "Smith, John").
First contributor inverted ("Family, Given"); subsequent contributors given-first.

NameSortOrder.description: Name sorting order for culturally appropriate collation.

oneOf.description:
Family name first (Western convention).
Example: "Smith, John" → "S" sorts before "T"

Given name first (Vietnamese convention).
Example: "Nguyễn Văn A" → "Nguyễn" sorts before "Trần"

NumberVariable.description: Known number variable keyword or custom kebab-case identifier.

OutputFormat.description: Output format for rendered citations and bibliographies.

oneOf.description: Plain text output.
HTML output.
Djot markup output.
LaTeX output.
Typst output.

placement.description: Where to place the label relative to names.

term.description: Locale term key for the role (e.g., "editor", "translator").

delimiter-precedes-last.description: When to use delimiter before last name.

min.description: Minimum number of names to trigger shortening.

subsequent-min.description:
Minimum number of names to trigger shortening on subsequent cites.
Defaults to `min` if not set.

subsequent-use-first.description:
Number of names to show when shortened on subsequent cites.
Defaults to `use_first` if not set.

use-first.description: Number of names to show when shortened.

use-last.description: Number of names to show after the ellipsis (et-al-use-last).

SimpleVariable.description:
Simple string variables.

Use `variable:` for string passthrough fields, even when the field name is
also present in [`NumberVariable`]. For example, `variable: volume` keeps the
source value as plain text, while `number: volume` opts into numeric
formatting behavior.

oneOf.description: Sort by reference type.
Sort by author/contributor.
Sort by title.
Sort by issued date.
Sort by custom field.

SortPreset.description:
Sort order presets for bibliography entries.

Each preset encodes the sort key sequence for a citation style family.
Use for the `bibliography.sort` field to avoid repeating boilerplate key lists.

oneOf.description: Author → year → title. Standard for author-date styles (APA, Chicago, Harvard).
Author → title → year. Used in some footnote and note styles.
Citation number only. Used in numeric styles (Vancouver, IEEE).

StyleInput.description:
A style reference that can be resolved locally or by an external resolver.

This union type allows callers to supply a style by local path, inline YAML,
or as an identifier that requires a remote resolver. Only `Path` and `Yaml`
can be resolved locally; `Id` and `Uri` require the citum-server resolver chain.

oneOf.description:
A style identifier to be resolved from registries (builtin, store, remote).
Requires external resolver chain — local resolution returns UnresolvedInput error.

A remote URI to be resolved (requires HTTP access).
Requires external resolver chain — local resolution returns UnresolvedInput error.
A local filesystem path to a YAML style file.
Inline YAML style definition.

SubsequentNameForm.description:
The contributor form used after the first integral mention in scope.

`Short` preserves non-dropping particles ("van Beethoven"); `FamilyOnly`
strips them ("Beethoven"). MLA-style narrative memory uses `Short`.

oneOf.description: Use the short contributor form for subsequent mentions.
Use family name only (without non-dropping particles) for subsequent mentions.

TemplateComponent.description:
A template component - the building blocks of citation/bibliography templates.

Each variant handles a specific data type with appropriate formatting options.

TemplateContributor.description: A contributor component for rendering names.

and.description:
Override the conjunction between the last two names.
Use `none` for bibliography when citation uses `text` or `symbol`.

contributor.description: Which contributor role to render (author, editor, etc.).

delimiter.description: Custom delimiter between names (overrides global setting).

form.description: How to display the contributor (long names, short, with label, etc.).

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for role-label agreement.

label.description: Optional role label configuration (e.g., "eds." for editors).

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

name-order.description:
Override the global name order for this specific component.
Use to show editors as "Given Family" even when global setting is "Family, Given".

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

shorten.description: Shorten the list of names (et al. configuration).

sort-separator.description: Delimiter between family and given name when inverted (overrides global setting).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateDate.description: A date component for rendering dates.

fallback.description: Fallback components if the primary date is missing.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateGroup.description:
A group component for grouping multiple components with a delimiter,
matching CSL 1.0 `<group>` semantics.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateNumber.description: A number component (volume, issue, pages, etc.).

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for number/ordinal agreement.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

show-with-locator.description:
When `true`, show this pages component even when a locator is present in a note-style citation.
By default, pages are suppressed in note-style citations when a locator is present.

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateTerm.description: A term component for rendering locale-specific text.

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for term selection.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

disambiguate-only.description:
When true, suppress this title component unless the reference needs
disambiguation (i.e. multiple works by the same author appear in the
document). Used by author-class styles (e.g. MLA) where the title
appears in citations only to resolve same-author ambiguity.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateVariable.description: A simple variable component (DOI, ISBN, URL, etc.).

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TermForm.description:
Form for term lookup.

Specifies which form variant of a term should be used in citation output.

oneOf.description: Long form of a term (e.g., "page" vs "p.").
Short form of a term (e.g., "p." vs "page").
Verb form of a term (e.g., "edited by").
Short verb form of a term (e.g., "ed." vs "edited by").
Symbol form of a term (e.g., "§" for section).

TextCase.description:
Text-case transform applied to title-like fields.

Styles select which transform applies to which field category.
The engine provides the generic primitives; styles own the selection.

oneOf.description: English headline-style title case (capitalize principal words).
Generic sentence case (capitalize first word only).

APA-style sentence case: capitalize first word of main title
and first word after each subtitle boundary.

NLM-style sentence case: capitalize first word of main title only;
subtitles preserve only explicit/protected capitals.
Capitalize the first letter of the value.
Transform the entire value to lowercase.
Transform the entire value to uppercase.
No transformation; render the value exactly as stored.
The primary title of the cited work.
Title of a book/monograph containing the cited work.
Title of a periodical/serial containing the cited work.

TypeSelector.description:
Selector for reference types in overrides.
Can be a single type string or a list of types.

VerticalAlign.description: Vertical text alignment relative to the baseline.

oneOf.description: Render at the baseline (default).
Render as superscript.
Render as subscript.

WrapConfig.description:
Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes applied around a rendered value.

Combines the wrap punctuation with optional text that appears inside the wrap
(between the wrap character and the rendered content).

inner-prefix.description: Text inserted after the opening wrap character but before the content.

inner-suffix.description: Text inserted after the content but before the closing wrap character.

WrapPunctuation.description: Punctuation to wrap a component in.

description: Parameter schemas for all Citum JSON-RPC methods.

format_document.description: Parameters for the `format_document` method (schema mirror of `FormatDocumentRequest`).

citations.description: Ordered citations as they appear in the document.

output_format.description: Output format (plain, html, djot, latex, typst). Defaults to plain.

refs.description:
Bibliography input as `RefsInput`: path (YAML/JSON/CBOR or `.bib`), inline YAML,
inline JSON, inline BibLaTeX (`{"kind":"biblatex","value":"@book{…}"}`) or legacy bare map.

style.description: Style identifier, path, URI, or inline YAML.

format_document.title: FormatDocumentParams

inject_ast_indices.description: Debug: embed AST node indices in output.

output_format.description: Output format for the rendered bibliography.

refs.description: Bibliography (references) as a map of reference objects.

style_path.description: Path to the Citum YAML style file.

citation.description: Citation object specifying which references to cite.

inject_ast_indices.description: Debug: embed AST node indices in output.

output_format.description: Output format for the rendered citation.

refs.description: Bibliography (references) as a map of reference objects.

style_path.description: Path to the Citum YAML style file.

title: Citum Server RPC

style_path.description: Path to the Citum YAML style file to validate.
